hard.next_step: 22 JUN - Siahn Bierman (Procurement Excellence Mgr) advised still going through requirement workshops, estimated ready to engage demos (stage 1) from mid July. 25 MAR - NBNCo will come to market with an RFI for S2C in the next couple of months. Zycus one of the invited responders. Tailspend and ANA.
hard.ais_why: ANA (autonomous negotiation, the sole AI SKU) is in the rep-captured RFI scope, a faint AI signal, but the note carries no buyer quote, no champion, no demo history, and BD engagement is Unengaged. Score 4 is a placeholder until the RFI lands.

north_star_verdict.headline: The S2C RFI Siahn Bierman promised for 'the next couple of months' on 25 Mar is now roughly 8 weeks overdue; NBN Co is still in internal requirement workshops with a mid-July demo the only forward marker. Deal is single-threaded through evaluator Chandru Ganesan, EB Emily Hall has stepped back after making the June intro, and no competitor, pain, or business case has surfaced yet.
north_star_verdict.math: 114 days to the 28 Oct close. In Qualified 109 days (since 19 Mar) with no Formal Eval or Shortlisted submission date recorded. Buyer's own 25 Mar guidance implied an RFI by roughly end-April; as of the 22 Jun next-step the RFI still has not issued and requirement workshops continue, an ~8-week slip. Mid-July demo readiness leaves ~15 weeks to run demos, an RFI response, shortlist, vendor selection, commercials, negotiation, and contracting into 28 Oct: aggressive but recoverable if NBN moves decisively after the workshops. Pace is behind a typical enterprise motion but not fatal given the runway.
evidence: Next step 22 Jun: Siahn Bierman advised requirement workshops still running, demo readiness mid-July (stage 1)
Next step 25 Mar: NBN to issue an S2C RFI 'in the next couple of months', Zycus an invited responder
LastActivityDate 21 Jun: email exchange with Siahn Bierman, deal is engaged (rep-side outreach awaiting momentum)
Task 11 Jun: Emily Hall (EB) introduced the Zycus team to Siahn Bierman then stepped back
Stage Qualified since 19 Mar (field history), 109 days, no Formal Eval or Shortlisted date
Contact role: Chandru Ganesan (Evaluator, primary) is the only named evaluator thread

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or financial target has been articulated by any NBN Co stakeholder in any field, task, or email. The rep-captured scope names 'Tailspend and ANA', implying the value themes are tail-spend leakage and AI/autonomous negotiation, but that is Zycus categorisation, not a buyer-stated number (source: SF next step 25 Mar, Products__c iContract;iSource;iManage;iSave;iSupplier;ANA). Siahn Bierman's requirement workshops (per the 22 Jun next step) are likely where NBN is building its internal case, but Zycus has not seen it. The move is to open the mid-July demo by asking Siahn and Chandru for their target savings, cycle-time, and tail-spend-under-management goals and anchor the ANA/Merlin ROI story to those figures.

economic_buyer.narrative: Emily Hall, Executive General Manager Procurement & Property, is the economic buyer by contact role and seniority (source: OpportunityContactRole). She is engaged at arm's length, not dark: on 11 Jun she personally replied to introduce the Zycus team to Siahn Bierman ('I will connect you with Siahn from my team', task 11 Jun), then delegated the evaluation to her procurement operations team. Since that intro there has been no commercial, pricing, or strategic-fit conversation with her, so sponsorship is passive rather than active. The read is a mapped-but-not-bought-in EB. The move is to convert her June facilitation into a short strategic-fit conversation on NBN's procurement transformation priorities before the RFI response locks.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Emily Hall, Executive General Manager Procurement & Property
Task 11 Jun: Emily Hall introduces Siahn Bierman

decision_criteria.narrative: The formal criteria are being written right now inside NBN's requirement workshops, which Siahn Bierman confirmed are still running as of 22 Jun and which will feed the S2C RFI (source: SF next step 22 Jun and 25 Mar). Zycus knows the functional shape of the evaluation (source-to-contract across iContract, iSource, iManage, iSave, iSupplier, plus ANA and tail-spend) from the captured scope, but the buyer's weighted priorities, must-haves, and AI expectations are not yet documented or shared. This is genuine pre-RFI timing, not a dead gap. The move is to influence the criteria while they are still soft: get in front of the workshop output and seed AI/autonomous-negotiation and tail-spend requirements the RFI should include.

decision_process.narrative: The process NBN is running is legible from the trail even without call transcripts: (1) internal requirement workshops (ongoing 22 Jun, led from Siahn Bierman's team), (2) a competitive S2C RFI to invited vendors including Zycus (promised 25 Mar, still not issued), (3) stage-1 demos from mid-July, then shortlist, deeper evaluation, vendor selection, commercials, and contracting toward the 28 Oct close (source: SF next step 25 Mar and 22 Jun). Emily Hall sits above the process as the procurement executive sponsor; Siahn Bierman owns the workshop stage and Chandru Ganesan is the primary evaluator. What is unconfirmed is the approval chain and probity governance, though Gareth Houston's 23 Mar note about 'probity and gifts/benefits' shows NBN runs a strict procurement-probity process. The move is to ask Siahn for the written RFI timeline and evaluation stages so the process is dated, not inferred.
sources: Task 23 Mar: Gareth Houston probity note

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec, and ERP/integration scope are entirely unknown at this Qualified stage and are appropriately far off (no MSA/SOW, procurement channel, or security-review signal in any field). NBN Co is a national broadband infrastructure operator, so a government-adjacent probity regime is already visible, Gareth Houston flagged 'probity and gifts/benefits' constraints on 23 Mar, which signals a formal, tightly governed procurement and contracting path once vendor selection happens. No SI or implementation partner is named. This is a knowledge gap that the RFI itself will begin to fill. The move is to probe NBN's ERP landscape, InfoSec review cycle, and preferred implementation model during the mid-July demo so delivery scope is understood before the RFI response.
sources: Task 23 Mar: Gareth Houston probity/gifts note
No SF contracting or InfoSec field data

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by any named NBN Co stakeholder. The product scope (source-to-contract plus tail-spend and ANA) implies the underlying pain is fragmented source-to-contract execution, poor tail-spend visibility, and manual negotiation, but no buyer has said this in their own words and there are no call transcripts to quote a pain owner (source: SF Products__c, SF next step 25 Mar). The requirement workshops are the buyer defining that pain internally right now, Zycus is outside them. The move is to use the mid-July demo to ask Siahn Bierman and Chandru Ganesan directly what is broken in NBN's current source-to-contract process, who owns it, and what the cost of not fixing it before their next fiscal cycle is.

champion.narrative: No champion or coach has emerged. Chandru Ganesan (Procurement Manager, primary evaluator) is the designated evaluation contact but shows neutral, process-only behaviour: no competitive intelligence shared, no internal navigation, no evidence he is advocating Zycus over other RFI responders (source: OpportunityContactRole; Outreach call 8 May could not connect). Siahn Bierman (Procurement Excellence Manager) owns the workshops and is the most responsive contact (email 21 Jun), but he is a neutral process owner with no demonstrated Zycus allegiance. Megan Cavill has a contact role but zero engagement record. Emily Hall facilitated the June intro but is not selling internally. The deal is single-threaded through a neutral evaluator with no internal advocate. The move is to develop Siahn or Chandru into a coach by delivering differentiated AI and tail-spend proof in the mid-July demo, then earn a warm intro deeper into the buying group.
sources: SF next step 22 Jun: Siahn Bierman
Task 8 May: Outreach call to Chandru could not connect

competition.narrative: The competitive field is unknown but confirmed to exist: NBN told Zycus on 25 Mar it will run a competitive S2C RFI and that Zycus is 'one of the invited responders', so this is a multi-vendor evaluation, not a sole-source (source: SF next step 25 Mar). No rival has been named in any field, next step, task, or email, and Competitors__c is blank, so Zycus is positioning blind ahead of the RFI. Given a Source-to-Contract mandate at a large Australian infrastructure operator, the likely field is the enterprise source-to-pay set, but naming a specific rival with no evidence would be fabrication. This is a real gate risk. The move is to ask Siahn or Chandru during mid-July demo prep who else is responding to the RFI, and to pre-seed ANA/Merlin AI and tail-spend depth as differentiators while the criteria are still forming.
sources: SF next step 25 Mar: multi-vendor RFI, Zycus invited

deal_movement.summary: The deal is inching, not stalling: the 25 Mar RFI-is-coming signal has not converted, but a fresh email thread (11-22 Jun) via Emily Hall's introduction of Siahn Bierman re-opened engagement and produced a concrete mid-July demo-readiness marker. No stage change since Qualified on 19 Mar; forecast held at Pipeline.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive field is undisclosed but confirmed live: NBN stated on 25 Mar it will run a competitive S2C RFI with Zycus as one invited responder, yet no rival has been named in any source and Competitors__c is blank. The single most important competitive fact is that Zycus is positioning blind, the RFI responder list is the first real read on the field. The early wedge is to seed ANA/Merlin AI and tail-spend depth into the criteria before they lock. No competitor is named because the evidence names none, not because the deal is sole-sourced.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: NBN's expectations are still forming inside its requirement workshops and have not been shared, so fit is inferred from the captured scope rather than confirmed against stated criteria. The functional footprint (source-to-contract plus tail-spend and autonomous negotiation) aligns squarely with the Zycus suite; the exposure is that AI expectations and probity/governance requirements are unquantified.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified deal with a live but competitive RFI ahead; the win levers are un-blinding the field, seeding AI criteria before the RFI locks, and building an advocate beyond a neutral evaluator.
flags: Un-blind the competitive field before the RFI locks: ask Siahn or Chandru who else NBN invited, so positioning is targeted not generic.
Convert a neutral evaluator into an advocate: give Chandru Ganesan or Siahn Bierman differentiated ANA / tail-spend proof they can carry internally.
Seed AI and autonomous-negotiation requirements into NBN's requirement workshops now, while criteria are still soft and pre-RFI.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early Qualified deal on a confirmed but still-unissued competitive S2C RFI at NBN Co; the deal is alive on a June email thread and a mid-July demo, but it is single-threaded through a neutral evaluator, the EB is passive, and the promised RFI is ~8 weeks overdue, so nothing is proven yet.

win_position.text: Zycus is a confirmed invited responder to NBN's upcoming S2C RFI with the full source-to-contract suite plus ANA in scope, so we are in the game before criteria lock (SF next step, 25 Mar).
The field is a competitive multi-vendor RFI and no rival has been named anywhere, so Zycus is positioning blind with no wedge yet (SF next step, 25 Mar; Competitors__c blank).
There is no champion and no quantified business case: Chandru Ganesan is a neutral evaluator and EB Emily Hall went passive after the June intro (OpportunityContactRole; Task, 11 Jun).

deal_momentum.text: The buyer-promised RFI ('next couple of months', 25 Mar) is roughly 8 weeks overdue with requirement workshops still running as of 22 Jun (SF next step, 22 Jun).
Siahn Bierman named a concrete mid-July stage-1 demo-readiness date, the only forward marker the buyer has offered (SF next step, 22 Jun).
Recent touches are rep-initiated emails (11-22 Jun) awaiting buyer pull, not buyer-driven momentum (SF activities, 11-22 Jun).

customer_commitment.text: No demo, POC, or pricing conversation has occurred and engagement is procurement-operations only, so there is no commitment signal yet (SF activities; stage Qualified since 19 Mar).
Emily Hall (EGM Procurement & Property) personally facilitated the intro to Siahn Bierman on 11 Jun, showing executive-level willingness to route the evaluation (Task, 11 Jun).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through a neutral evaluator with no internal advocate leaves Zycus blind to the field and unable to influence criteria (OpportunityContactRole; SF next step, 22 Jun).
NBN runs a strict procurement-probity regime (Gareth Houston declined event travel on 23 Mar citing gifts/benefits governance), so the process will be formal and slow (Task, 23 Mar).
The 28 Oct close requires compressing an unissued RFI, demos, shortlist, selection, commercials, and contracting into ~15 weeks (SF close date).

champion_support.evidence: No champion; single-threaded through neutral evaluator Chandru Ganesan, no advocacy (OpportunityContactRole; SF next step 22 Jun).

buyer_pull.evidence: Recent activity is rep-initiated email; buyer-promised RFI ~8 weeks overdue (SF activities and next step, Jun).

competitive_clarity.evidence: Confirmed multi-vendor RFI but no rival named; positioning blind (SF next step 25 Mar).

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer preference expressed. Engagement is neutral and process-only ('as your in this research phase'); no Zycus-favourable comparison and no Zycus-specific next step requested (SF activities, Jun).

business_case.evidence: No ROI, savings, or automation impact articulated by NBN; scope names 'Tailspend and ANA' but no numbers (SF next step 25 Mar; Products__c).
